description: Evaluation scenarios for the Stochastic Consensus & Debate research skill

scenarios.name: Next.js Performance Optimization
scenarios.invocation: /research "How should I optimize this Next.js application for performance?"
scenarios.question: How should I optimize this Next.js application for performance?

must_have: At least one concrete disagreement surfaced (not all recommendations identical)
Performance angle produces specific bottleneck findings (not generic tips)
Contrarian angle challenges at least one mainstream optimization assumption
Recommendations use 'do X when Y' format, not generic 'consider X'
Consensus section identifies findings that 3+ researchers independently reached
must_not_have: Confidence defaulting to HIGH without supporting evidence
Recommendations that are mutually exclusive without noting the conflict

quality_signals.HIGH: Researchers disagree on at least one recommendation (e.g., SSR vs SSG vs ISR), synthesizer resolves with 'depends on' logic that specifies the conditions
quality_signals.MEDIUM: Multiple perspectives present but disagreements are not fully resolved
quality_signals.LOW: All researchers converge to the same generic list (bundle size, images, caching) without genuine debate

scenarios.name: tRPC vs GraphQL vs REST Comparison
scenarios.invocation: /research "Compare tRPC vs GraphQL vs REST for this project"
scenarios.question: Compare tRPC vs GraphQL vs REST for this project

must_have: Genuine disagreements surfaced between at least 2 researchers
Technology-choice angle preset applied (maturity, community, performance, DX, contrarian)
Each technology appears in at least one researcher's findings with specific evidence
Disagreements classified as factual vs values-based
Synthesizer produces conditional recommendation (e.g., 'use tRPC when full-stack TS; GraphQL when multi-client')
Risk/Security angle addresses API surface area and schema exposure risks
must_not_have: Synthesizer declaring a single winner without conditional logic
All researchers agreeing unanimously (this topic has genuine tradeoffs)
Recommendations not tied to codebase characteristics observed during research
Missing mention of team size or type-safety considerations

quality_signals.HIGH: Synthesizer identifies a factual disagreement (e.g., performance benchmarks) AND a values-based disagreement (e.g., type-safety importance), resolves each differently
quality_signals.MEDIUM: Tradeoffs listed but not resolved into conditional recommendations
quality_signals.LOW: Generic comparison table that could apply to any project without codebase-specific insight

scenarios.name: Microservices Testing Strategy
scenarios.invocation: /research "What's the best testing strategy for a microservices architecture?"
scenarios.question: What's the best testing strategy for a microservices architecture?

must_have: Practical and theoretical angles both represented (not purely academic)
Contrarian angle challenges a standard assumption (e.g., 'integration tests are overrated', or 'contract tests add complexity that small teams can't sustain')
At least one recommendation specific to the number/size of services in this codebase
Risk/Security angle covers test coverage gaps that create security blind spots
Consensus section includes at least one finding (e.g., unit tests are always needed)
Disagreement between angles on testing pyramid vs honeycomb vs trophy shape
must_not_have: Recommending the same testing strategy regardless of team size or service count
Ignoring contract testing / consumer-driven contracts entirely
Security angle treating testing strategy as purely functional (missing security test coverage)
All agents agreeing on a single canonical model without surfacing real-world objections

quality_signals.HIGH: Researchers disagree on integration test strategy (e.g., full-stack vs contract vs mocking), synthesizer classifies as values-based (team velocity vs confidence) and gives conditional resolution based on team size
quality_signals.MEDIUM: Multiple test levels covered but without genuine conflict or conditional recommendations
quality_signals.LOW: Generic 'unit + integration + E2E' recommendation that applies to any architecture

evaluation_protocol.how_to_run: Invoke the skill with each scenario's invocation string, then check the output against pass_criteria
automated_checks: Recommendations use actionable language (do/use/prefer/avoid), not hedging (consider/maybe/might)
manual_checks: At least one recommendation is conditional ('when X, do Y' format)
Confidence assessment is calibrated (not always HIGH)
Individual perspectives section shows genuinely distinct insights per angle
No credentials or sensitive data visible in output

scoring.pass: All automated checks pass + majority of manual checks pass
scoring.fail: Any automated check fails OR less than half of manual checks pass
